source: Old-School Essentials Advanced Fantasy Players Tome
notes: Armour proficiency is normalized for app use into light/medium/heavy/shields. source_text preserves the printed armour line.
Spells are number of memorized/prepared spells by spell level. 0 means no spell slot.
THAC0 and bracketed ascending attack modifier are both stored.
Hit dice strings omit the printed asterisk; constitution_bonus_applies=false marks post-name-level fixed hp increments where OSE says CON modifiers no longer apply.
v2: Adds a proficiencies object with armor and spell_list data; preserves armor_proficiencies for backward compatibility.
v2: Adds xp_bonus_rule for prime requisite XP bonus automation.
v2: Treats listening at doors / hear noise as a skill-like roll and adds listen_at_doors to class skill progressions.
v2: Adds feature_text_raw with class feature text from the source entry; detailed rules are not further extracted unless they already existed as progression tables.
v3: Adds spellcasting_notes as sheet-facing raw notes extracted from class feature text; feature_text_raw is retained for audit/reference.
v3: Normalizes proficiencies.spell_list to include id, magic_type, and source_text for spellcasting classes.
v3: Adds skill_notes for all skills available to each class, including default move_silently, listen_at_doors, and search_secret_doors for all classes.

expertise_points.notes: Optional expertise points: 4 points at 1st level, then 1 point at each later level.

listen_at_doors.notes: Roll succeeds if result falls within listen_at_doors range.

percent_skills.notes: Applies to percentage-valued skills in skills_by_level.

move_silently.note: Default attempt available to all characters; class skill percentage supersedes default if present.

search_secret_doors.note: Default search/secret door detection available to all characters; race/class features may alter this.

skill_notes.text: May attempt to climb sheer surfaces or vertical obstacles; use the listed class skill value.

skill_notes.text: May reduce falling damage as indicated by the class table.

skill_notes.text: May attempt to hide in shadows; use the listed class skill value.

skill_notes.name: Listen At Doors
skill_notes.text: May listen at doors or similar barriers. Use the listed class/race value; thief/assassin hear noise progression is normalized here for table use.

skill_notes.name: Move Silently
skill_notes.text: May attempt to move quietly when avoiding notice. All characters may try; class skill values, if present, supersede the default procedure.

skill_notes.name: Search Secret Doors
skill_notes.text: May search for secret doors and similar hidden features. All characters may try; race/class detection features may improve or add special cases.

skill_notes.text: May walk along tightropes, narrow beams, and ledges; use the listed class skill value.

Activating Mental Powers
A duergar must spend a round con-
centrating in order to activate a mental
power. While concentrating, the duergar
may not move, attack, or perform any
other actions.
In combat: Like spell casting, activating a
mental power can be disrupted in combat
(see Spell Casting under Combat, p234).
The player must declare the use of a men-
tal power before initiative is rolled.

Stealth
Underground, duergars have a 3-in-6
chance of moving silently.

After Reaching 9th Level
A duergar has the option of creating an
underground stronghold that will attract
duergars from far and wide. Duergars
usually live in clans, so duergars of the
character’s clan will be attracted to live
under their roof. Duergars from other
clans will generally be friendly and may
collaborate in times of war or disaster.
A duergar ruler may only hire duergar
mercenaries. Specialists and retainers of
any race may be hired.

spellcasting_notes: Arcane Magic: See Magic for full details on arcane magic.

Spell casting: Illusionists carry spell books containing the formulae for arcane spells. The level progression table shows both the number of spells in the illusionist’s spell book and the number they may memorize, determined by experience level. Thus, a 1st level illusionist has one spell in their spell book, selected by the referee, who may allow the player to choose.
Spell list: Illusionists use the illusionist spell list.

Magical research: An illusionist of any level may spend time and money on magical research. This allows them to add new spells to their spell book and to research other magical effects. When an illusionist reaches 9th level, they are also able to create magic items. Researched spells, effects, and magic items must be within the scope of the illusionist class, as judged by the referee.

Using magic items: As spell casters, illusionists are able to use magic scrolls of spells on their spell list. They can also use items that may only be used by arcane spell casters. They are not able to use magic items restricted to magic-users.

spellcasting_notes: Arcane Magic: See Magic for full details on arcane magic.

Spell casting: Magic-users carry spell books containing the formulae for arcane spells. The spell casting chart shows both the number of spells in the magic-user’s spell book and the number they may memorize, determined by experience level. Thus, a 1st level magic-user has one spell in their spell book, selected by the referee, who may allow the player to choose.
Spell list: Magic-users use the magic-user spell list.

Magical research: A magic-user of any level may spend time and money on magical research. This allows them to add new spells to their spell book and to research other magical effects. When a magic-user reaches 9th level, they are also able to create magic items.
Using magic items: As spell casters, magic-users are able to use magic scrolls of spells on their spell list. They can also use items that may only be used by arcane spell casters.

skill_notes.name: Climb Sheer Surfaces
skill_notes.text: May attempt to climb sheer surfaces or vertical obstacles; use the listed class skill value.

skill_notes.name: Find Remove Treasure Traps
skill_notes.text: May find or remove treasure traps; use the listed class skill value.

skill_notes.name: Hear Noise
skill_notes.text: Advanced listening/hear-noise skill. In this dataset, also normalized into listen_at_doors for sheet use.

skill_notes.name: Hide In Shadows
skill_notes.text: May attempt to hide in shadows; use the listed class skill value.

skill_notes.name: Listen At Doors
skill_notes.text: May listen at doors or similar barriers. Use the listed class/race value; thief/assassin hear noise progression is normalized here for table use.

skill_notes.name: Move Silently
skill_notes.text: May attempt to move quietly when avoiding notice. All characters may try; class skill values, if present, supersede the default procedure.

skill_notes.name: Open Locks
skill_notes.text: May open locks; use the listed class skill value.

skill_notes.name: Pick Pockets
skill_notes.text: May attempt pick-pocketing or similar sleight of hand; use the listed class skill value.

skill_notes.name: Search Secret Doors
skill_notes.text: May search for secret doors and similar hidden features. All characters may try; race/class detection features may improve or add special cases.
